tragedy history part parcel this section Vancouver Island's west coast.
More than ships have gone down this part ocean, known "The Graveyard Pacific".
most tragic sinking 1906 steamship Valencia.
Rescuers from shore nearby vessels were unable reach ship, when went down, passengers crew members perished.
following year federal government began construction lighthouse Pachena Boat lifesaving trail along this coastline.
trail followed rugged telegraph route built 1890, thus connecting lighthouses Cape Beale Carmanah Point with other lighthouses towns towards Victoria.
Between Bamfield Carmanah Point, distance 47km (29mi) metre foot) wide trail built, that shipwrecked sailor could reach coastal communities with ease.
From 1907 1912, team constructed route from Bamfield Pachena Point, from this point became impractical continue.
From Pachena Point Carmanah Point route metre (4.9 foot) wide trail.
South here continued original telegraph line trail.
With modern navigation communication, trail became obsolete, overgrown vegetation.
Only trail Pachena lighthouse maintained.
1960's interest re-open trail grew.
1973, Parks Canada started reconstruction.
section from Pachena Carmanah Point rebuilt, while (18.6 portion from Carmanah Port Renfrew, most primitive, upgraded.
completed 1980.
